expectations.text: Produces at least 3 distinct user stories
expectations.evidence: Produces 5 distinct stories: 'Leave a rating and comment on a lesson', 'Edit my lesson feedback shortly after posting', 'Delete my lesson feedback shortly after posting', 'See aggregated feedback for my course on the dashboard', 'Hide a learner comment that violates content policy'.

expectations.text: Uses the 'I can' phrasing (not 'I want' or 'I need')
expectations.evidence: Every story uses 'I can' (e.g., 'I can leave a star rating and an optional written comment', 'I can edit the rating and comment I left'). No 'I want' or 'I need' appears in any narrative.

expectations.text: Every story has an explicit Title field separate from the Connextra narrative
expectations.evidence: Each story begins with a bolded '**Title:**' line (e.g., '**Title:** Leave a rating and comment on a lesson') preceding the 'As a / I can / so that' block.

expectations.text: Uses Connextra format: every story contains 'As a', 'I can', and 'so that'
expectations.evidence: All five stories use the tri-part Connextra format with explicit 'As a', 'I can', and 'so that' lines.

expectations.text: Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner
expectations.evidence: Each story lists all six principles individually with a verdict: '**I**ndependent — pass/fail: ...', '**N**egotiable — pass: ...', '**V**aluable — pass: ...', '**E**stimable — pass: ...', '**S**mall — pass: ...', '**T**estable — pass: ...'. Verdicts differ per story (e.g., Edit/Delete stories are marked 'I — fail').

expectations.text: Names concrete roles (learner, admin) — does not use generic 'user'
expectations.evidence: Uses 'course learner' for learner-facing stories and 'course admin' for admin-facing stories. No generic 'user' appears in any narrative.

expectations.text: Handles the web-first/mobile-later platform constraint explicitly
expectations.evidence: Preamble: 'kept the scope to **web only** per the "mobile can come later" note'. Closing note: 'Mobile is deliberately not represented; when it's picked up, clone the learner-side stories with a platform qualifier.' Story 1's What Changed also repeats: 'Scoped to web per the PM's "web first" direction'.

expectations.text: Does not add Acceptance Criteria (no AC section or bullet list of measurable requirements) — ACs are out of scope
expectations.evidence: No 'Acceptance Criteria' section or AC bullet list. The response only mentions AC to explain what was deliberately kept out of the narratives (e.g., '500-char cap out of the narrative — those are acceptance criteria').
